# datadog_cost_custom_forecast (Resource)

Provides a Datadog Cost Custom Forecast resource. This resource manages the custom forecast override entries for a `datadog_cost_budget`. **Note:** each entry's `(month, tag_filters)` combination must correspond to an existing entry on the referenced budget, and the budget must exist before this resource is created.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A budget with two monthly entries for the same tag combination, plus a
# custom forecast override for the first month. The entry's (month, tag_filters)
# must match one of the budget's own entries.
resource "datadog_cost_budget" "example" {
  name          = "Engineering Q1 Budget"
  metrics_query = "sum:aws.cost.amortized{service:ec2} by {service}"
  start_month   = 202601
  end_month     = 202602

  entries {
    amount = 1000
    month  = 202601
    tag_filters {
      tag_key   = "service"
      tag_value = "ec2"
    }
  }
  entries {
    amount = 1200
    month  = 202602
    tag_filters {
      tag_key   = "service"
      tag_value = "ec2"
    }
  }
}

resource "datadog_cost_custom_forecast" "example" {
  budget_uid = datadog_cost_budget.example.id

  entries {
    amount = 900
    month  = 202601
    tag_filters {
      tag_key   = "service"
      tag_value = "ec2"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `budget_uid` (String) The UUID of the budget that this custom forecast belongs to. Changing this value forces a new resource to be created.

### Optional

- `entries` (Block Set) Monthly custom forecast entries. Each entry overrides the forecast for one `(month, tag_filters)` combination that must already exist as a budget entry. To remove all custom forecast entries, destroy this resource rather than setting an empty `entries` set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--entries))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Timestamp the custom forecast was created, in Unix milliseconds.
- `created_by` (String) The ID of the user that created the custom forecast.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the custom forecast set.
- `updated_at` (Number) Timestamp the custom forecast was last updated, in Unix milliseconds.
- `updated_by` (String) The ID of the user that last updated the custom forecast.

<a id="nestedblock--entries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `entries`

Required:

- `amount` (Number) The forecast override amount for the month. Value must be at least 0.000000.
- `month` (Number) The month the entry applies to, in `YYYYMM` format.

Optional:

- `tag_filters` (Block List) Tag filters that scope this entry to a specific budget entry tag combination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--entries--tag_filters))

<a id="nestedblock--entries--tag_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `entries.tag_filters`

Required:

- `tag_key` (String)
- `tag_value` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Cost custom forecasts are imported using the parent budget's UUID, e.g.
terraform import datadog_cost_custom_forecast.example a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890
```
